usage:
psl - Protocol Specification Language tool

Usage:
  psl <command> [options] [arguments]

Commands:
  list                     List all available protocols
  show <protocol>          Show protocol field structure
  decode <protocol>        Decode binary data from stdin to JSON
  encode <protocol> <json> Encode JSON to binary, output to stdout
  validate <file.psl>      Validate a PSL file
  pcap <file.pcap>         Decode packets from pcap/pcapng file
  generate <lang> <proto>  Generate code from protocol definition
  diagram <protocol>       Generate RFC-style protocol diagram
  doc <protocol>           Generate protocol documentation
  lsp                      Start PSL language server

Generate Options:
  --all             Generate for all protocols
  -o <dir>          Output directory (default: current)
  --pkg <name>      Package name (default: proto)
  --template <file> Use custom template

Diagram Options:
  --format <fmt>    Output format: ascii (default), svg
  -o <file>         Write to file

Doc Options:
  --all             Generate for all protocols
  --format <fmt>    Output format: md (default), html
  -o <dir>          Output directory

Pcap Options:
  -p <protocol>     Filter by protocol name
  --filter <expr>   Filter by field value (e.g. "srcPort==80")
  --format <fmt>    Output format: summary (default), json, table

Options:
  -l <file.psl>   Load additional PSL file before executing
  -f <file>       Read input from file instead of stdin/argument
  -o <file>       Write output to file instead of stdout
  --raw           Show raw PSL source (for show command)
  --lang <en|zh>  Set output language (default: en)
  -h, --help      Show this help message
list.col.name: Protocol
encode.no_input: provide JSON as argument or use -f <file>
show.col.name: Field
show.msg.name: Message
